name: South American Vegetable Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 large peeled garlic clove, bashed and chopped
2 cups peeled thin-skinned potatoes, roughly chopped (such as Yukon Gold)
4 fresh husked tomatillos, cut into eighths
14 1/2 ounces no salt added whole tomatoes, diced in juice
1 cup canned black beans, rinsed and drained
2/3 pound peeled Chayote squash, cored, roughly diced
6 ounces pattypan squash slices, roughly diced
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the oil in a large saucepan over medium high heat and fry the onion until it becomes translucent, about 2 minutes.
2. Toss in the garlic, oregano, allspice, cumin, and potatoes; cook another 3 minutes, stirring so the garlic doesn't brown.
3. Pour in enough stock to release any flavorful bits that might have stuck to the pan. Stir in the remaining stock, tomatillos, and tomatoes. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer briskly for 15 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender.
4. Add the beans, corn, cactus squash, squash slices, chipotle sauce, and salt. Return to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 5 minutes.
5. Ladle into bowls and sprinkle cilantro on top.
